finding_results.notes: Kline keeps enforcement language but now varies register under pressure with contractions and concrete floor context (front-desk backlog/lobby load), reducing pure policy-mouthpiece cadence while preserving authority.
Eli's terms-sequence assent lines were expanded into substantive commitments and consequence-aware responses, eliminating repeated one-word acknowledgments and improving leverage/ownership cadence.

summary: Applied targeted dialogue/idiolect/cadence revisions for both packet findings with minimal collateral churn: Kline's authority voice now shows pressured human variation, and Eli's Theo terms exchange now avoids transcript cadence through substantive, character-true responses.
